### Post by thinklife on 2006-12-10
Hi,
I hope to have direct rendering,but when i type glxinfo in the terminal it shows me "direct rendering:no".As my graphic card is made by S3 graphics company(im not so sure about the specs),I hope to get a driver for it.Can anyone tell me where or how?Thanks!!:) 
Heres my xorg.conf:

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"vesa"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51

	Identifier	"Default Screen"

EndSection

	Mode	0666
EndSection

        Option  "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

### Post by pay on 2006-12-10
The S3 driver is built into the kernel but i'm not sure if it is enabled by default. If it isn't try```
sudo su
cd /usr/src/linux
aptitude install build-essential
make menuconfig
```Then go ```
Device Drivers --> Character Devices --> 
  <M> /dev/agpgart (AGP Support) 
  Direct Rendering Manager (XFree86 DRI support)  --->
    <M>   Savage Video Cards
```
and then compile the kernel```
make clean
make
make modules_install
make install
```

---

### Post by thinklife on 2006-12-11
Thanks For the Reply.I solved it and now direct rendering is on.
I found out the chipset of my S3 graphic card by typing"lscpi" into the terminal,and found out that the chipset is savage.Then i replaced the driver"vesa"with "savage" in xorg.conf.After a reboot the problem is solved :)

---
